programs.name: Yoga with Baby
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength in this class specifically designed for new caregivers. Here you can relax knowing participation is at your own pace and you can attend to your baby as needed. The recommended age for your baby is 1 to 12 months.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve you overall endurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Instructor Recert Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This in-person recertification course focuses on the competencies learned in the instructor, coach and examiner courses and provides a refresher to those competencies. Course content will be updated every two years. This course provides the 4 points required to complete the leadership recertification for instructors, coaches, examiners and examiner mentors. This course does not automatically recertify a candidate. Following successful course completion candidates must complete the recertification process to be recertified.Prerequisite: Current Swim Instructor and/or Lifesaving Instructor and/or Examiners and/or any Coach Certification. Candidates must bring their instructor course materials to this recert course. These may include: Swim For Life Award Guide or Toronto Learn to Swim Manual, Instructor Manual, Swim Patrol Award Guide, Bronze Medals Award Guide, First Aid Award Guide, Teaching Swim for Life.Note: This course can be completed at any time during your 24 month currency period and leadership recertification online form submitted with payment when your awards are expiring.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED - Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). This recertification course provides a review of first aid and CPR skills. Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Intermediate First Aid (CSA Z1210:24).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Philpott Tennis
programs.description: Free tennis  instructions available for beginner and intermediate participants ages 13+.  Offered at various sites in priority neighborhoods across the city. The program  is run by qualified instructors hired and supervised by Philpott Children’s  Tennis and all equipment is provided. Participants will use 25-27- inch  racquets, green dot balls and play on full court. Participants in this program may be  encouraged to volunteer for the program, coaches can also choose candidates  from this program to join the Leadership program with Philpott Children’s  Tennis. Philpott Children’s Tennis teaches their core values to participants to  help encourage them in their challenges of sport and life. programs.name: Soccer: Recreational with Caregiver
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: SPLASH Swim Team
programs.description: Expand your swimming skills by practicing your strokes in a team-based environment and showcase your skills during competitive swim meets. Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths. IMPORTANT: The City of Toronto is dedicated to ensuring the safety of all individuals who participate in its activities and services. This program requires the review of Ontario's Concussion Awareness Resources and the City of Toronto's Concussion Code of Conduct. A signature of acknowledgment is required for participants under the age of 26, or parents/caregivers of participants under the age of 18, on the first day of program participation.Visit www.toronto.ca/concussionsafety for more information on concussion safety in our sports and recreation programs.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Tennis
programs.description: Calling all tennis fans! Through practice and play, you will build on tennis-specific conditioning and coordination while learning game rules and developing fundamentals including serving, forehand and backhand. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your tennis skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp. Equipment: Campers must bring their own tennis racquet.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Visual Arts
programs.description: In this camp you are the artist. You will explore a wide variety of art mediums and learn the secrets of painting, sketching, drawing and more. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your artist skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Cardio Dance
programs.description: Cardio dance is a dynamic and exciting aerobic workout that anyone can enjoy. With a focus on various culturally inspired music and dance movements and a goal of lifting your heart rate you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Cardio Dance
programs.description: Afro and Soca fusion beats and rhythms inspire this high-intensity cardio workout. Using fast steps and full-body movements like squats, jumps and body waves, you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Cardio: High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Cardio: Kickboxing
programs.description: Calling all fitness warriors. This high-intensity cardio workout that incorporates non-contact punches, kicks and athletic moves will help you improve your strength, balance and coordination.

programs.name: Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: Dance: Ballroom
programs.description: Get ready to glide across the dance floor with ease. Learn basic steps, postures and holds of various dance styles like the waltz, foxtrot, tango, rumba and quick step through various exercises and engaging choreography.Partners are not required as dancers will be partnered in this class as needed.

programs.name: Dance: Caribbean
programs.description: Let rhythm and music move you with Caribbean dance! Focusing on fluidity and expression, learn Soca and jazz-style movements through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance

programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.description: Young people who love nature will enjoy this combination of gardening and outdoor exploration. programs.name: Ranger Patrol
programs.description: The Ranger Patrol program focuses on performance with a 200-metre timed swim and 100-metre fitness medley. It also provides instruction on assessing unconscious victims, treatment for shock and obstructed airway procedures.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Rookie Patrol.

programs.name: Ranger Patrol

It also provides instruction on assessing unconscious victims, treatment for shock and obstructed airway procedures.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old and successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 9 or Rookie Patrol.

programs.name: Rookie Patrol
programs.description: The Rookie Patrol program focuses on performance with a 100-metre timed swim and 350-metre workouts. programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Cross and Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and  physically. Judgement, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of  water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire  the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on,  and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant  lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres  in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered  in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Cross and Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and  physically. Judgement, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of  water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire  the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on,  and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant  lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres  in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered  in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

programs.name: Bronze Star
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: Gentle Fit
programs.description: Seeking a class with a more relaxed pace to improve your fitness? Using gentle movements and low-intensity level exercises, workouts will include both  cardio and muscle conditioning options.

programs.name: Intro to Poetry
programs.description: Learn to experiment creatively with words, rhythm, and the basic rules of poetry in these introductory workshops. Students will be given the tools to cultivate inspiration, express themselves freely and partake in the cultural art form that is poetry. No prior writing experience required.

programs.name: Karate - Advanced
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Meditation
programs.description: Meditation is a practice of mental well-being. Combining basic meditation techniques with restorative breathing you will learn to relax the mind and body.

programs.name: Meditation
programs.description: Meditation is a practice of mental well-being. Combining basic meditation techniques with restorative breathing you will learn to relax the mind and body.

programs.name: Mobility and Strength: Chair
programs.description: Improving your mobility and strength can help make day-to-day activities easier. With a focus on balance and coordination you will use a chair or your walker to complete basic movements in a safe and engaging environment.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification or Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This recertification course offers candidates with certification that will be expiring the opportunity to maintain current certification at the National Lifeguard level in a pool setting. Your lifeguarding skills will be tested based on physical standards, judgment and skill.Prerequisite: National Lifeguard - Pool (current or expired).Equipment: Bring a whistle.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Nature Poetry
programs.description: Celebrate summer's arrival, and with it, the ability to further connect with the great Canadian landscapes. In these handson poetry workshops, students will be invited to notice and creatively express about their connection with natural surroundings. With the Guild Park as a sparkling backdrop of curiosity and imagination, poets will harness nature's energy through petry. No prior writing experience required.

programs.name: Philpott Tennis

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 3
programs.description: Build endurance in the water and improve your swimming skills. Develop entries, surface dives and stroke improvement. Learn to support an object in the water. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Youth 2.

programs.description: Camp comes to you in this virtual version of Adapted CampTO. programs.name: Workshop: Hair (Caregiver and Tot)

Equipment: You must bring your own tools and hair accessories.

programs.name: Workshop: Jewellery Making

programs.name: Badminton: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational badminton. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgement, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

programs.name: Bronze Star
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skills  and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire  self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze  Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a  fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.
